natural_strengths: Building coalitions that make reform politically sustainable
Balancing ambitious vision with pragmatic implementation
Recruiting and elevating talent regardless of background
Treating education and culture as strategic infrastructure
Consultative decision-making that builds genuine buy-in
natural_weaknesses: Gradual reform can become indefinite postponement of hard decisions
Consultative processes can mask decision avoidance
Pragmatic balance may slide toward pure power preservation
Foreign talent recruitment may undermine developing domestic capability
communication_style: Consultative and intellectually curious. Gathers input from diverse sources before deciding. Communicates decisions with clear reasoning. Maintains extensive networks for continuous learning.
one_liner: I shall be an autocrat: that is my trade. And the good Lord will forgive me: that is his.
